title: Use-after-free window when replacing `self->wrapped` and other proxy fields in C extension
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

In `src/wrapt/_wrappers.c`, every site that replaces an instance field on a proxy object (`self->wrapped`, `self->args`, `self->kwargs`, `self->instance`, `self->wrapper`, `self->enabled`, `self->binding`, `self->parent`, `self->owner`) decrements the old value's refcount *before* assigning the new value to the field. The pattern is uniformly:

```
Py_INCREF(value);
Py_XDECREF(self->field);   /* may run the old object's __del__ */
self->field = value;       /* not reached until after __del__ returns */
```

This creates a reentrancy window: if the `Py_XDECREF` drops the old object's last reference, its `tp_dealloc` runs synchronously, and any Python code reachable from that finalizer (`__del__`, weakref callbacks, GC triggered by allocation inside the finalizer, logging hooks, audit hooks) that touches the proxy will read `self->field` while it still points at the now-freed object. The result is a use-after-free observable as a crash, an attribute lookup against garbage memory, or — most insidiously — a successful read of stale data from a memory region that has not yet been overwritten.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
